rule: Long rooms and galleries should keep a clear circulation spine with detail on the side walls.
implementation_clauses: place displays, lamps, shelves, or plants along edges
avoid bulky furniture in the path
vertical or corridor elements get lighting and guard/detail markers without blocking movement

rule: Social rooms need a furniture cluster that faces either a view, fireplace/focal wall, or conversation center.
implementation_clauses: anchor seats around a shared center
leave a clear path around the cluster
tie at least one seat direction to the main view or focal wall
seating, table, rug, and light form a social island while center circulation remains legible

rule: Every important room needs an identity stack: focal wall, functional anchor, storage/display layer, textiles/plants, and layered lighting.
implementation_clauses: give each room one focal wall or task wall before adding loose decor
combine functional anchor + storage/display + lighting + soft detail
keep circulation clear through the room center or along one edge

rule: A vertical accent must act as an arrival marker or view anchor, not just extra height.
implementation_clauses: place the vertical element near entry, stair, corner, or public axis
make approach paths reveal the landmark
use roof cap, light slot, or facade rhythm to finish the silhouette

rule: Terrain must become part of the architecture through rock/earth plinths, retaining edges, planting pockets, and stepped arrival.
implementation_clauses: build a layered stone/earth base before placing the main shell
use height bands, retaining edges, and planting pockets instead of a flat lot
connect the entry path to the raised terrain plinth with visible steps or ramps

rule: Preserve mined interior clause circulation-threshold-marker when matching room function and style.
Preserve mined interior clause circulation-wayfinding-light when matching room function and style.
Preserve mined interior clause template-circulation-spine when matching room function and style.
Preserve mined interior clause universal-clear-circulation when matching room function and style.
Living rooms should be public view rooms with a social cluster, focal wall, and clear arrival edge.
implementation_clauses: face seating toward the main view or focal wall
leave a clear path from entry to social core
add display, plant, and layered lighting details

rule: Foreground gardens should be composed as rooms and approach scenery, not scattered plants.
implementation_clauses: reserve a foreground zone before the facade
organize path, hedge/planting beds, rocks, lights, and small water/seat details as a sequence
frame the main entry or public room view with paired planting pockets

rule: Interior atmosphere needs layered lighting: ambient, task/focal, and accent points.
implementation_clauses: combine ceiling/wall light with task light near anchors
avoid one isolated torch as the only light source
multiple lights mark task zones, corners, or room center rather than one bare lamp

rule: Entries should be transition hubs that reveal the main room, stair, landmark, or view.
implementation_clauses: connect entry to public room and vertical circulation

Preserve mined interior clause living-rug-anchor when matching room function and style.
Water-edge buildings need a public-room threshold: deck, reflection edge, view window, and planting transition must align.
implementation_clauses: place living/dining/lounge rooms on the water/view side
connect public rooms to a deck, terrace, pier, or reflection basin
use planting and low edge framing so water is part of the scene

rule: Preserve mined interior clause template-clear-center when matching room function and style.
Bedrooms should be quiet rooms anchored by a sleep niche and soft side details.
implementation_clauses: place bed on the quiet side
avoid blocking the room path with bed placement

rule: Corridors and galleries are circulation first, with detail rhythm along edges.
implementation_clauses: use side-wall lamps, alcoves, display, or windows
do not use bulky furniture in the path

rule: Studies should pair a library/display wall with a desk or reading anchor and controlled light.
implementation_clauses: place desk near a window or quiet wall
keep the center compact and uncluttered

Preserve mined interior clause universal-three-layer-lighting when matching room function and style.
Flat walls must be broken with repeatable micro-depth layers: stairs, slabs, rails, panes, lights, columns, or recesses.
implementation_clauses: add trim bands around windows and roof edges
use slabs/stairs for shadow lines and rail blocks for edges
repeat detail rhythm by facade bay instead of random decoration

rule: Display walls should act as focal surfaces for studies, halls, galleries, and ceremonial rooms.
implementation_clauses: cluster books, banners, paintings, shelves, or objects on one readable wall
keep the opposite circulation side clearer
display and ceremonial objects collect on a visible focal edge

rule: Large glass must be tied to a view axis, public room, and exterior scene.
implementation_clauses: assign large glass to living/dining/lounge or stair reveal zones
avoid using glass uniformly on every wall
place seating, counters, or circulation so the glass reads as a destination

rule: Studies and libraries need a focus wall of books/display plus a quiet work or reading anchor.
implementation_clauses: place desk or seating so it reads as a working/reading zone
books and desk/display objects create a readable study focal wall

rule: Kitchens should be functional service rooms with work wall, storage, prep surface, and task light.
implementation_clauses: group work blocks by wall or island
keep a short link to dining/living
separate service clutter from the best view wall

rule: Layered eaves should create a readable silhouette and protect the facade rhythm.
implementation_clauses: stack roof/eave bands instead of one flat cap
use overhangs to mark floor or room hierarchy
coordinate eave layers with entry, porch, or tower rhythm

rule: Storage rooms should read as organized utility bays with repeated modules and an access lane.
implementation_clauses: place containers/shelves in rows or wall bands

Workshops should group heavy task blocks, tools, storage, and task light along robust surfaces.
implementation_clauses: anchor workbench/anvil/furnace-like blocks to a wall

rule: Preserve mined interior clause study-archive-storage when matching room function and style.
Preserve mined interior clause study-reading-light when matching room function and style.
Kitchens need a compact work wall or island sequence with prep, heat, storage, and light.
implementation_clauses: group furnace/crafting/counter/storage into one work band
keep the center or service path clear
add task lighting above the work band
cooking, prep, storage, and task lighting form a compact wall band

rule: A flat roof should read as a usable terrace with access, parapet, furniture, and view edge.
implementation_clauses: include stair or internal access logic to the roof terrace
build a parapet/rail edge and at least one seating or planter cluster
align the terrace with view, water, garden, or vertical accent

rule: Courtyard or patio voids should become internal scenery that rooms face and use.
implementation_clauses: leave a readable open void or patio in the plan
orient quiet rooms or social rooms toward the void
add planting, water, seating, or floor texture inside the void

rule: Bathrooms should be compact wet zones with clear door access and a fixture wall.
implementation_clauses: keep bathroom smaller than primary living rooms

rule: Secondary wings should be offset to form terraces, courtyards, or framed views rather than random protrusions.
implementation_clauses: offset a secondary wing from the main bar
use the gap as a patio, entry court, or water-facing deck
keep the wing program legible: service, quiet room, garage, or lounge

rule: Tower rooms must prioritize vertical access, lookout windows, compact storage, and small anchors.
implementation_clauses: place lookout windows or display at edges

rule: Storage should form an organized wall or bay instead of scattered containers.
implementation_clauses: group chests/barrels/shelves into a wall band
pair storage with task or display lighting
storage blocks repeat along an edge while preserving an access aisle

rule: Bathrooms and wet zones should use compact fixture walls with semi-private access.
implementation_clauses: group basin/cauldron-like fixtures along one wall
add small lighting and floor material contrast
wet fixture and counter elements stay compact on one service edge

rule: Bedrooms read best when the bed is held in a niche/corner with paired bedside and soft light details.
implementation_clauses: anchor bed to a quiet wall or corner
keep bed away from exposed glass/view edges unless it is framed
bed anchors a private wall or corner with nearby light and storage

rule: Ceremonial halls should use symmetry, axis, elevated focal blocks, banners/candles, and open center space.
implementation_clauses: put ceremonial/detail blocks at the end or sides

rule: Plants work best as corners, view softeners, or threshold details rather than random clutter.
implementation_clauses: place plant clusters near windows, corners, or entries
use them to soften glass and hard edges
plants sit in corners or view edges as part of a garden/window response

rule: Long-bar massing should organize circulation and view rooms along one readable direction.
implementation_clauses: align the long side with the main view or garden
put public rooms along the view bar and service rooms in a side/back band
break the bar with terrace, porch, or wing offsets
